top_signals.title: Stocks End Choppy Day Little Changed as Yields Steady; SOX Drops
top_signals.summary: The S&P 500 Index ended a choppy session on Monday near where it started trading, with swings in oil prices setting the tempo during the day as investors parsed mixed signals around US-Iran peace talks. Ten-year Treasury yields steadied below 4.6%, while semiconductor stocks traded lower.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Some Buyer Fatigue in Credit: Matt Brill
top_signals.summary: Matt Brill, head of North American investment grade credit at Invesco, joins Scarlet Fu and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss high-grade credit, inflation's impact on fixed income, and the Invesco Total Return Bond ETF (Ticker: GTO).  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
